channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: AEW stars Adam Copeland, Christian Cage talk Pure Plank, how planking revitalized their lives
item.description: All Elite Wrestling stars Adam Copeland and Christian Cage, whose real name is Jay Reso, launched and developed Pure Plank. They talked to Fox News Digital about the equipment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michigan's 7-foot newcomers earn themselves a nickname after their dynamic Big Ten debut
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Michigan has a nickname for its pair of 7-foot newcomers: Area 50-1. The tag was unveiled by the school on social media Tuesday night after Vladislav Goldin and Danny Wolf produced otherworldly numbers in a 67-64 victory at No. 11 Wisconsin to open the Big Ten schedule.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Iowa State and SMU athletic directors in a 'stay off my lawn' moment regarding latest rankings
item.description: Athletic directors at Iowa State and SMU are in a turf war over the latest College Football Playoff rankings, leading to a “stay off my lawn” moment on social media.
item.dc:creator: Eddie Pells, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Goldin helps Michigan beat No. 11 Wisconsin 67-64 as Badgers take their 1st loss
item.description: Vladislav Goldin scored a career-high 24 points – including three straight baskets inside down the stretch – and Michigan defeated No. 11 Wisconsin 67-64 in the Big Ten opener for both teams.
item.dc:creator: Steve Megargee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Miami's playoff hopes nosedive as Alabama rises in the latest College Football Playoff rankings
item.description: Miami's playoff hopes took an all-but-final nosedive while Alabama's got a boost Tuesday night in the last rankings before the 12-team College Football Playoff bracket is set next weekend. The Hurricanes (10-2) moved down six spots to No.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Padres catcher Kyle Higashioka signs with Texas Rangers
item.description: Free agent catcher Kyle Higashioka and the Texas Rangers agreed Monday night on a $13.5 million, two-year contract that includes a mutual option for the 2027 season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Robbie Gould resigns as head football coach at Rolling Meadows High School
item.description: District 214 officials have announced the resignation of former Chicago Bears kicker and Rolling Meadows Mustangs head football coach, Gould, after a 5-5 season.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Signing with Dodgers was really easy decision for 2-time Cy Young winner Blake Snell
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Two-time Cy Young Award winner Blake Snell says it was a really easy decision to sign with the World Series champion Los Angeles Dodgers and the presence of three-time MVP Shohei Ohtani played a part, too.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Michigan coach Sherrone Moore fires offensive coordinator Kirk Campbell after unit struggles in 2024
item.description: ANN ARBOR, Mich. (AP) — Michigan coach Sherrone Moore has fired offensive coordinator Kirk Campbell. Moore made the move on Tuesday, three days after the Wolverines closed the regular season with a13-10 upset win over rival and then second-ranked Ohio State.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Religious beliefs put two captains in spotlight during Premier League's Rainbow Laces campaign
item.description: The captains of two Premier League teams are in the spotlight for their choices as the competition celebrates LGBTQ+ inclusion in its campaign to promote equality and diversity. Rainbow-colored captain armbands were issued to the 20 clubs for matches last weekend and the current midweek round.
item.dc:creator: STEVE DOUGLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Analysis: Getting benched may have been best thing that happened to Bryce Young, Anthony Richardson
item.description: On Football analyzes the biggest topics in the NFL from week to week. For more On Football analysis, head here. ___ Getting benched may have been the best thing that happened to Bryce Young and Anthony Richardson. Both second-year quarterbacks are playing well since returning to the starting lineup.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ESPN star calls Browns' Deshaun Watson 'albatross,' wonders how team would've fared without him
item.description: With Jameis Winston proving he can make Kevin Stefanski's Browns offense work, ESPN's Troy Aikman called Deshaun Watson an "albatross," wondering how the team would've done without him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Rays move to Ybor City could still be possible
item.description: For months, the Tampa Bay Rays celebrated the signing of a deal between their group, the city of St. Petersburg, and the Pinellas County Commission.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Patterson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Father of ex-Cowboys star Leighton Vander Esch arrested for attempted strangulation, police say
item.description: Former Dallas Cowboys star Leighton Vander Esch's father was arrested in Idaho early Sunday morning on charges of attempted strangulation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Transgender powerlifter asks Minnesota Supreme Court to let her compete in women's events
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — A transgender athlete should be allowed to compete in the women's division at powerlifting events because she's protected against discrimination by the Minnesota Human Rights Act, her attorneys urged the state Supreme Court on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texans' Azeez Al-Shaair suspended 3 games without pay after violent hit on Trevor Lawrence
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Houston's Azeez Al-Shaair was suspended by the NFL without pay for three games Tuesday for repeated violations of player safety rules following his violent hit to the head of Jacksonville's Trevor Lawrence, which led to a concussion.
item.dc:creator: KRISTIE RIEKEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Browns' Jameis Winston prays for the 'Lord to deliver' him from pick-sixes after throwing 2 in their loss
item.description: Cleveland Browns quarterback Jameis Winston reacted postgame to his performance on Monday night, when the 30-year-old threw two pick-sixes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fueled by boos, Browns receiver Jerry Jeudy turns in 235-yard performance in return to Denver
item.description: DENVER (AP) — The boos only brought out the best in Jerry Jeudy as he returned to Denver. "They only boo you,” Jeudy reasoned, “when they know there’s something great in you.
item.dc:creator: PAT GRAHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: BYU runners use olive oil to win NCAA championship
item.description: The school's coach said his runners used “an old-school running trick” by rubbing olive oil on their legs to keep themselves warm.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: 2024 PPR All 51 Team
item.description: 2024 PPR All 51 Team: Each season our selection committee recognizes the best players from across the county, assembling our "All 51" team. This list includes our first team picks, and honorable mentions.
item.dc:creator: Maddy Worley, Fox 5

item.title: McCaffrey injury potentially rules him out for rest of season
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — San Francisco running back Christian McCaffrey suffered a knee injury that coach Kyle Shanahan described as “potentially” season-ending in the 49ers’ 35-10 loss to the Buffalo Bills on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Chicago Sky, Angel Reese to open 2025 season against Indiana Fever, Caitlin Clark
item.description: Angel Reese and Caitlin Clark won't have to wait long to renew their rivalry during the next WNBA season.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Mitchell scores 35, Cavs end Celtics' winning streak with 115-111 win in matchup of East's elite
item.description: Donovan Mitchell scored 20 of his 35 points in the fourth quarter as the Cleveland Cavaliers ended Boston's seven-game winning streak with a 115-111 victory over the Celtics on Sunday night in the second intense matchup between the Eastern Conference’s top teams this season.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Big Ten fines Michigan and Ohio State $100,000 each for postgame melee
item.description: ROSEMONT, Ill. (AP) — The Big Ten Conference announced it fined Michigan and Ohio State $100,000 each for violating the conference's sportsmanship policy for the on-field melee at the end of the Wolverines' win in Columbus on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: BYU eliminated from Big 12 contention, beats Houston
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - BYU learned it was eliminated from Big 12 Championship Game contention at halftime, but the Cougars still had a job to do. Jake Retzlaff ran for two touchdowns and threw for 167 yards to lead No. 19 BYU to a 30-18 victory over Houston on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Montrose def. Pueblo West 18-7 to advance to the championship game
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)— The Montrose Red Hawks are heading to the Class 4A state championship game after defeating the Pueblo West Cyclones 18-7 in a hard-fought semifinal matchup.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Gus Malzahn is leaving UCF to become Florida State's offensive coordinator, AP source says
item.description: Gus Malzahn is resigning as Central Florida's head coach to become Florida State's offensive coordinator, a person familiar with the hire told The Associated Press on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Aggies blow 17-point fourth quarter lead in loss to CSU
item.description: FORT COLLINS, Colo. (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah State football team could not hold a 30-13 fourth quarter lead in its season finale at Colorado State, and the Aggies end the season with a 42-37 loss to the Rams.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Former Tennessee head coach Bill Battle dies at 82
item.description: KNOXVILLE, Tenn. (WATE) -- Former University of Tennessee Coach William "Bill" Battle III died on Thanksgiving morning at the age of 82, according to the university.
item.dc:creator: Hope McAlee, NewsNation

item.title: Lions beat Bears 23-20 to extend win streak to 10 games
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Jared Goff threw two touchdown passes to Sam LaPorta, Jake Bates made three field goals in the first half and the Detroit Lions held off the Chicago Bears 23-20 on Thursday to extend their winning streak to 10 games. The Lions snapped a seven-game losing streak on Thanksgiving.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jon Batiste, Ledisi, Trombone Shorty and Lauren Daigle to perform during Super Bowl pregame
item.description: The Super Bowl pregame will have some Louisiana flavor: Multi-talented performer Jon Batiste will hit the stage to sing the national anthem, while Trombone Shorty and Lauren Daigle are slated to perform “America the Beautiful.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: San Jose State is disappointed Boise State forfeited but looks forward to volleyball final
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — San Jose State said Thursday it was disappointed Boise State forfeited its semifinal match in the Mountain West women's volleyball tournament but that the Spartans were focused on the future.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Caleb Williams still poised to break Bears QB records despite roller coaster rookie season
item.description: Caleb Williams has shown poise and promise as the Chicago Bears quarterback, despite the team's struggles, and is on track to break franchise records for passing yards and touchdowns.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Orlando takes on Chicago, aims for 4th straight win
item.description: Chicago Bulls (8-11, ninth in the Eastern Conference) vs. Orlando Magic (12-7, third in the Eastern Conference) Orlando, Florida; Wednesday, 7 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
